page: Google Cloud Platform m2-ultramem-208 is a memory-optimized server instance designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. It features 208 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon processors running at 2.7 GHz, mapped to 104 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is equipped with 5888.0 GB of RAM, providing approximately 28.31 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning storage must be provisioned externally.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests falling into the bottom 25%. However, the massive core count enables top-tier multi-core CPU performance, placing it in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core tests and the top 25% for PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, with cached small-block read operations reaching top-tier speeds and large-block read and write operations scoring in the top 25%. Redis SET operations also achieve top-tier throughput.

The primary tradeoff of the m2-ultramem-208 is its lower single-threaded efficiency and lack of local storage, which requires relying on network-attached storage. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this instance represents a high-density resource allocation tailored for workloads where massive memory capacity is the primary bottleneck rather than raw single-core speed. It is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, massive Redis deployments, enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-bound data processing pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform m2-ultramem-208 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance configured with 208 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs operating at 2.7 GHz and an extensive 5888.0 GB of RAM. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPUs. Benchmark data highlights a significant contrast in performance: while single-core metrics are weak, multi-core CPU performance and cached memory bandwidth rank in the top 10%. Large-block memory read and write speeds and PassMark CPU scores are also strong. The primary tradeoff is lower single-threaded efficiency, making it less suitable for sequential processing. Qualitatively, the m2-ultramem-208 offers high resource density for memory-bound architectures. It is best suited for large-scale in-memory databases, massive Redis caching layers, and enterprise-grade data processing workloads requiring substantial memory capacity.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m2-ultramem-208 is a memory-optimized instance with 208 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 5.75 TB of RAM, designed for massive in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m2-ultramem-208 features 208 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 5.75 TB of RAM, optimized for large-scale in-memory database workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring massive RAM capacity and strong multi-core performance for enterprise in-memory databases and caching.
bullet_points: Equipped with 208 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 5888.0 GB of RAM
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and cached memory bandwidth
Achieves top-tier throughput for Redis SET operations
Lacks bundled local storage and integrated GPU hardware
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise ERP systems
